skill.name: bib-formatter
skill.description: Convert reference lists and in-text citations between RIS, BibTeX, plain text, and CSL-JSON, triggered when you need to unify bibliography/citation styles before journal submission or compare before/after formatting differences.
skill.summary: Automated bibliography converter supporting RIS, BibTeX, plain text, and CSL-JSON with automatic journal style matching.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 103 static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The 13 high-severity 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings flag argparse description strings, YAML front matter, and CSL XML element text, none of which involve cryptographic operations. The 54 medium-severity 'external commands' findings flag markdown backticks used for documentation code examples and RIS field name formatting, not executable code. Network calls to zotero.org and filesystem writes for output files are legitimate academic tool operations. No malicious intent or actual security vulnerabilities were identified.

content.user_title: Format academic bibliographies and citations across journal styles
content.value_statement: Researchers waste time manually reformatting bibliographies for different journal submission guidelines. This skill automates bibliography conversion between RIS, BibTeX, plain text, and CSL-JSON formats with automatic journal style matching from the Zotero repository.
actual_capabilities: Convert bibliographies between RIS, BibTeX, plain text, and CSL-JSON formats
Format reference lists using CSL journal styles (Nature, Lancet, NEJM, and more)
Auto-retrieve journal CSL styles from the Zotero style repository
Generate formatted in-text citations for specific reference IDs
Detect and report missing metadata fields in bibliography entries
Produce before/after comparison tables for formatting verification
limitations: Requires Python 3.10+ with rispy, bibtexparser, requests, and citeproc-py packages
Plain text input produces minimal output with only title information
CSL style auto-retrieval requires internet access to the Zotero repository
Does not support every bibliography format (e.g., Endnote XML or Medline)

use_cases.title: Journal submission preparation
use_cases.description: A researcher has references in BibTeX format and needs to reformat them to NEJM style for submission.
use_cases.target_user: Academic researchers preparing manuscripts for journal submission

use_cases.title: Multi-source reference consolidation
use_cases.description: A graduate student collects references from multiple databases in RIS format and needs uniform formatting.
use_cases.target_user: Graduate students consolidating references from different sources

use_cases.title: Citation verification and cleanup
use_cases.description: A peer reviewer checks that all in-text citations follow the correct journal style.
use_cases.target_user: Peer reviewers and editors checking citation accuracy

prompt_templates.title: Format bibliography from BibTeX file
prompt_templates.prompt: Format my bibliography file refs.bib using the Nature journal style and save the output as formatted.txt.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher needs to convert a BibTeX bibliography to a formatted reference list

prompt_templates.title: Convert RIS references to Lancet style
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this RIS bibliography to The Lancet style and show me a before and after comparison.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher needs to reformat RIS references for a specific journal

prompt_templates.title: Generate in-text citations for specific entries
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate formatted in-text citations for ITEM-1 and ITEM-2 from my CSL-JSON file using NEJM style.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher needs formatted citations for specific references in a paper

prompt_templates.title: Validate and fix incomplete bibliography entries
prompt_templates.prompt: Check my bibliography file for missing fields and list which entries need author, year, or DOI information.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher needs to identify and fix incomplete references before formatting

output_examples.input: A BibTeX file with 3 journal article references in various formats
output_examples.output: A formatted reference list following Nature journal style with numbered citations. Each entry includes author names, article title, journal name, volume, issue, pages, and DOI where available. A before/after comparison table shows the original and formatted versions.

output_examples.input: A plain text list of 2 reference titles
output_examples.output: Formatted bibliography entries with sequential numbering. Since plain text input provides only titles, the output shows the original title formatted according to the selected CSL style with a note that enriched metadata would produce better results.

best_practices: Use structured formats (RIS, BibTeX, CSL-JSON) instead of plain text for the best formatting quality
Always specify the input format explicitly instead of relying on auto-detection
Verify critical metadata fields (author, year, title, journal, DOI) before the final export
anti_patterns: Do not paste raw unformatted reference text and expect formatted output without providing structured input
Do not skip the before/after comparison review, since formatting errors can introduce citation mistakes
Do not rely on auto-retrieved CSL styles without confirming they match the target journal guidelines

faq.question: What bibliography formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports RIS, BibTeX, plain text, and CSL-JSON input formats.

faq.question: Do I need to install any software?
faq.answer: Yes, you need Python 3.10+ and the packages rispy, bibtexparser, requests, and citeproc-py.

faq.question: Can I use a custom citation style?
faq.answer: Yes, provide a local CSL file using the --style option for any custom style.

faq.question: How does the journal style auto-retrieval work?
faq.answer: The tool searches the Zotero style repository using the journal name and downloads the matching CSL file.

faq.question: Does this work offline?
faq.answer: Yes, if you already have the CSL style files saved locally. The auto-retrieval requires internet access.

faq.question: Why is my output missing some information?
faq.answer: Missing metadata in the input file causes incomplete output. Fill in missing author, year, DOI, or other fields and run again.
